title: The Classroom What is its Function
display_title: The Classroom What is its Function
wikitext:
[]: The Classroom: What is its Function?             Quote Post by mechashiva » Sat Oct 25, 2014 12:42 pm I blame the forum heading being the of the classroom because it reminded of something that bugged me a little in my second watch of Evangelion: Knowing that SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Oct 25, 2014 2:39 pm the teacher is just senile and is basically given free rein to talk about whatever

[]:             Quote Post by Aoren » Sat Oct 25, 2014 3:04 pm I don't see relation between the teacher speech/lie * about the second impact and the Marduk Institute pilot selection. In the sense of "he said this because of this particular thing". It's more like showing how the government lies about the second impact to the society. The classroom is essential to show the social aspect of the characters and background characters as this is the place where they converge and socialize, but no more than that. The classroom don't play a role either in the end of the series or The End of Evangelion. At least not a relevant one. ** It's usually used to: Show how the government hides the truth of the second impact to the people. i.e Most of the time the teacher is talking about the meteorite that fell in Antarctica, now when Toji comes back to the class after being selected as the 4th child he says something like "Yes, come in. I am aware" to him. Show Shinji's failure to socialize. i.e Making friends at first. Progress caring-relationships between the characters. i.e Shinji and Rei, Shinji and Asuka, Toji and the Hikari. Show the fear of the society. i.e As they leave the city. I see it being used to display things. At least I don't see any hidden message. * : I said "speech/lie" because I can't state whether the teacher was lying on purpose from the very beginning or if Marduk/Nerv told him to lie when they selected the 4th child (and thus the teacher had also been deceived till then). ** : In any case the teacher lied to the students. Shinji knew it from the beginning. In the end of the series an alternate reality is shown with Misato as the teacher, now why is that? Well, I think Shinji relate the real teacher with concealment and secrets, and this alternate reality is used to demonstrate how Shinji can be happy even without being an Eva pilot. In the "Eva world" the real teacher represents the society being deceived. In the alternate reality the teacher is long gone, so, what stops you from being happy Shinji? He can't blame the control over society anymore (maybe he being controlled with lies). The world can be a happy place. He replies lying to himself: "everyone hates me", and the series goes on to that topic.

[]:             Quote Post by mechashiva » Sat Oct 25, 2014 6:40 pm The classroom is essential to show the social aspect of the characters and background characters as this is the place where they converge and socialize, but no more than that. The classroom don't play a role either in the end of the series or The End of Evangelion. At least not a relevant one.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Oct 25, 2014 7:07 pm The Marduk Institute doesn't exist, remember? It's just a front put up by Nerv.

[]:             Quote Post by mechashiva » Sat Oct 25, 2014 7:08 pm Yeah, I forgot.

[]:             Quote Post by Aoren » Sat Oct 25, 2014 8:09 pm mechashiva wrote: It just struck me that if they're teaching the same thing in every scene where the teacher has dialogue that the idea of schooling the pilots was sort of a formality but its also teaching as the insemination of mis-information.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Oct 26, 2014 5:24 am Aoren wrote: There is a big empty space in the community about why Toji was designated, as far as I know one can never know.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Nov 05, 2014 8:13 am ElMariachi wrote: Sakura who is still in the hospital.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Nov 05, 2014 8:51 am There was a semi-fanon name (Maki? Mari?) , but it was definitely not Sakura. Monk Ed wrote: I always wanted to call her Hanako, cuz I think that's a pretty and less overused name.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Nov 05, 2014 10:01 am Touji's sister is called Mari in a fanfic I once glanced at.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Nov 05, 2014 12:49 pm Monk Ed wrote: Touji's sister is not named Sakura in NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Nov 05, 2014 4:42 pm Eh. We do know what you mean, but it is technically incorrect since it describes NTE Touji's sister.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Nov 06, 2014 3:57 am EGF, where technicalities about the name of a character across universes of the franchise is serious business!
